creator: karl@pielorz.com
text:
Current version of Cacti pkg (cacti-0.8.8g_1) doesn't appear to install / depend on 'php56-mysql':

'pkg info -d cacti' shows a dependancy on 'php56-mysqli'.

With a fresh 10.3-R system, doing 'pkg install cacti' (and then setting up Apache + PHP) results in a system that gives you a blank screen when you try to access the Cacti index.php screen.

Strangely - no errors are given or logged, but having traced through the code - this appears be to because 'php56-mysql' is not installed.

Installing 'php56-mysql' fixes the problem - visiting the index.php page after installing that package, cacti walks you through the '/install' setup - and then works.

-Karl
